title: Git Commit Conventions
desc: Commit message rules for this repo. English only — Korean wastes 2-3x tokens in git history.
steps: Language: English only — no Korean in commit title or body
Format: '<type>: <what changed and why>' — one line title under 72 chars
Types: feat / fix / refactor / test / docs / bump / chore
feat     = new user-visible capability
fix      = bug fix (describe symptom, not file name)
refactor = restructuring with no behavior change
test     = test additions or corrections
docs     = documentation only (CLAUDE.md, README, comments)
bump     = version bump (always 3 files: AssemblyVersion, WKAppBot.csproj, HelpAndAliases)
chore    = build/tooling/CI, no production code change
Body: bullet points for non-obvious details only — skip if title is self-contained
- Use '- ' bullets, not numbered lists
- Explain WHY, not WHAT (the diff already shows what)
- Max 5 bullets; if more needed, consider splitting the commit
NO file listings — never list changed files in title or body
Bad:  'fix: TeeTextWriter.cs, Program.cs, EyeProxy.cs 수정'
Good: 'fix: --sudo no longer spawns a visible console window'
Co-Authored-By line: always append 'Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>'
Examples:
feat: multi-browser CDP port scan auto-routes ask to correct Chrome instance
fix: elevated Eye proxy drops OperationCanceledException on rapid reconnect
fix: webbot window position wrong when sandbox path creates new tab
refactor: split TeeTextWriter error record into per-tag last-value map
bump: v5.11 → v5.12 (WebBot multi-port scan + errors.jsonl healing log)
chore: add git commit conventions skill
author: claude
